reasoning: Re-opened WSGUNS.MAC:954-1002 (the TYP$LV 'LIVE SHELL HAS COLLISIONS ALLOWED?' block). The ROM shell-vs-player test is: LDD M.XT / LSLD ('SCALE BACK UP TO REGULAR COORDS', ×2) / BMI 50$ (behind you => skip); then LDU M$VX+M.S1 / CMPU #200 / IFGE SUBD M$VX+M.S1 ELSE SUBD #200 ('MIN DISTANCE LEST SHOTS ARE TOO LARGE'); BLE 40$; else SUBD #100+10 ('MAX GUN SPEED PLUS FUDGE') / IFLE. Radix check: #200=$200=512, #100+10=$110=272 (bare hex, left-to-right); minimum closing step = max(M$VX,$200)+$110 => $310 in the slow-ship case, larger for a fast ship — so the finding's '~$310 units of closing distance/frame' and 'a faster ship eats shells from further out' are both correct arithmetic. This is a forward-DEPTH swept threshold gated ADDITIONALLY by a screen-cursor-reach envelope (lines 971-986: BJ.CX/BJ.CY vs VGCURR/VGCURL/VGCURT/VGCURB *4 — a shell beyond cursor reach branches to 50$ and cannot hit you at all), then GNHTSG (shot down if laser on & aimed) or GNAHIT ('ELSE IT SURELY HIT US'). Ours (verified sim.ts:302-323, COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS=80 at state.ts:252) resolves the same event by a static world-space sphere `collides(s.pos, COCKPIT, COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS)` about the origin. The collision MODEL genuinely differs (depth-sweep + player-speed dependence + cursor-reach gate vs static origin sphere). The finding is honestly scoped: it does NOT claim 80 is wrong by a stated factor (M.XT is a ×2-scaled perspective-adjacent quantity, not cleanly convertible), and notes the divergence is mostly invisible because our fireballs home on-axis (G-001) into the sphere. Held as an accepted, not-fully-decoded model simplification.

Read WSCPU's COLLISION section myself (WSCPU.MAC:339-446). The ONLY routine there is CPHTSA:: ('SPACE LAZAR HIT ALIEN SHIP') — the player's laser hitting the alien (INC A$DMC, DEC A$HTA, explode via XPSA, or throw-ship-for-a-loop for Darth). There is NO reverse routine: nowhere does an alien SHIP body reaching the player deduct anything. The only 'hit us' path in space is WSGUNS.MAC:996-997 ('ELSE IT SURELY HIT US' / JSR GNAHIT), and GNAHIT (WSGUNS.MAC:1137, 'ALIEN GUN HIT PLAYER') is reached only from VWGUN for a LIVE gun-shell. Confirmed by grep: across WSGUNS/WSCPU/WSGAS the only player-damage entry is GNAHIT; the shield drain itself (WSGAS DO1GAS, DEC S.GAS at :76) is gated on GS.GLW/GS.HIT set by that gun-hit path, not by any body test. So the ROM lets a TIE fly through you harmlessly — only its cannon fire hurts. Ours (sim.ts:308-315, verified) additionally does damage++ / player-death when a TIE reaches COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS. In our model 'lives' ARE shields (state.ts:296 'a genuine collision still costs a shield', :680 lives:STARTING_LIVES), so the finding's 'costs a shield' is accurate. Genuine DIVERGENCE, and it is a documented deliberate deviation (state.ts:288-299, story 9-3 AC#3) that only fires for a near-dead-center TIE (TIEs peel at TIE_NEAR_BOUND=0x800 before reaching the 80-unit sphere, so it rarely triggers). Factual claim holds; accept disposition is well-founded.
corrections: Cross-batch reconciliation with pair-tie-ai A-010 (per coverage-review §2b): A-010 and G-010 describe the SAME behaviour, cite the SAME code (sim.ts:308-315), share the SAME class (DIVERGENCE), and rule OPPOSITE (A-010 fix, G-010 accept). My primary-source read resolves the shared fact — the ROM has no TIE-body-vs-player collision (verified above) — in BOTH their favour, so neither should ship as 'refuted'. Adopt G-010's `accept`: it is the better-reasoned of the two (it weighs A-011's near-bound peel, which A-010 does not) and it is a documented intentional deviation (story 9-3 AC#3). Demote A-010 to a cross-reference of G-010; do NOT hand back a refuted-A-010 next to a confirmed-G-010 on the identical behaviour.

reasoning: Re-opened WSLAZR.MAC:100-135 (TSTLAZ). On the fire-button edge: SEI / LDA VG.LON / IFNE / INC LZ.ALT / CLR LZ.HIT / LDB #8 / STB LZ.EDG / CLR VG.LON. Then LDA LZ.EDG / IFGT / DEC LZ.EDG / STA LZ.ON — so the laser is 'on' (collision-active) for an 8-game-frame sweep, decremented one per frame, restarted by a fresh edge. Radix: #8=8 decimal; 8/20.508=0.39 s per sweep — matches. Crucially I decoded the deferred operand the finding could not: VG.LON is generated in WSINT.MAC:175-192 by strict EDGE detection — VG.LSN=current fire bits, EORA VG.LSO / ANDA VG.LSO => VG.LSE ('1==>FALLING EDGE (WENT ON)'), and VG.LON is set ONLY when VG.LSE has the FIRE bit (a fresh press transition), then cleared each frame. So the ROM trigger is strictly SEMI-AUTOMATIC: one 8-frame sweep per distinct trigger pull, NO auto-repeat while held. Ours (verified sim.ts:156-174): `let fireCooldown = state.fireCooldown - dt; if (input.fire && fireCooldown <= 0) { ...fire...; fireCooldown = FIRE_INTERVAL }` — LEVEL-triggered auto-fire that re-fires every FIRE_INTERVAL=0.25 s (state.ts:165) for as long as input.fire is held. The divergence (edge-driven sweep vs fixed-seconds auto-fire cooldown) is real and now firmer than the finding claimed. Held.
corrections: Strengthening (not a defect in the finding, which honestly scoped itself as 'lower-confidence / cannot pin the interval from WSGUNS/WSLAZR/TCSPLS alone'): the divergence is not merely an unpinned re-fire interval — the firing MODEL differs. Decoding VG.LON at WSINT.MAC:188-192 shows the ROM fires exactly one shot per trigger falling-edge with no auto-repeat (semi-auto), whereas ours auto-fires at 4/s while the button is held (level-triggered). So the accurate divergence is 'ROM edge-triggered semi-auto, 8-frame sweep per pull' vs 'ours level-triggered 0.25 s auto-fire'. This also means the 8-frame LZ.EDG value is the laser's on/collision DURATION per shot, a different quantity from a re-fire interval — do not port 8 frames as a cooldown.

reasoning: Re-opened TCMES.MAC:549 with the message-macro decoded. `.MESS X,Y,C,<text>,name` stores the ASCII arg literally via .ASCIN /.../ (macro def TCMES.MAC:486-500) — these are plain ASCII strings, NOT indexed glyphs. Line 549 verbatim: `.MESS -248.,0480.,VJMFL,<PULL TRIGGER TO START>,STR`. So the cabinet's start invitation is literally 'PULL TRIGGER TO START' (STR). Grepped the shipped message tables (TCMES/TCHSCR/WSMAIN): there is NO 'PRESS START' string anywhere — confirmed. Ours draws 'PRESS START' at render.ts:993 (attract) and again at render.ts:1020 (game-over), both verified. Genuine text-content divergence; a one-line swap. Held. (Disposition note: our control is a key/click so 'PULL TRIGGER' is aspirational, but the authentic ROM wording is objectively STR.)

Re-opened TCMES.MAC:604-605 and TCHSCR.MAC:56-70 (VWHSCR). Line 605 verbatim: `.MESS -320.,0316.,RED,<PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE>,RF2` — decoded via the .MESS/.ASCIN macro (literal ASCII), this is the hi-score board header. VWHSCR MESSON's it: when NOT entering initials it posts MS.RF2 ('REBEL FORCE TOP OF SCREEN', TCHSCR.MAC:65) over the whole-screen table (THSY2). So the player-facing high-score board is titled 'PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE'. Ours draws the generic 'HIGH SCORES' at render.ts:1034 (drawHighScoreBoard, verified). Core divergence holds. Held.
corrections: Two sub-claim inaccuracies in the finding, corrected from primary source: (1) 'RF1 = <REBEL FORCE>' is not supported — there is NO literal <REBEL FORCE> message in the table. TCMES.MAC:604 is `.NEXTMESS -320.,000.,VJMFL,RF1`, and .NEXTMESS (macro def TCMES.MAC:512-525) writes XY+color for a message SLOT but emits NO .ASCIN text of its own — it re-positions the FOLLOWING message's text. So RF1 is the SAME 'PRINCESS LEIA'S REBEL FORCE' header re-centred for the half-screen initials-entry layout (VWHSCR posts MS.RF1 'REBEL FORCE MIDDLE OF SCREEN' + THSY1 when UPDFLG is entering initials, TCHSCR.MAC:59-63), not a distinct short string. The comments' 'REBEL FORCE' is shorthand. (2) 'There is no HIGH SCORES string in the ROM message table' is imprecise: there IS `<RESET HIGH SCORES>` at TCMES.MAC:657 — but it is an OPERATOR SERVICE-MENU option (adjacent to RESET TIMING INFO / RESET OPTIONS / TEST NOVRAM / STARTING SHIELD), never a player-facing board title. The finding's conclusion is right for the player-facing title; the absolute 'no HIGH SCORES string' overstates it.

reasoning: Re-opened TCMES.MAC:600-603 (the HS1..HSZ initials-screen block) with the .MESS/.ASCIN macro decoded (literal ASCII). Verbatim: :600 `<MESSAGE FROM REBEL COMMAND POST>,HS1`, :601 `<YOU ARE A TRUE REBEL PILOT>`, :602 `<THE FORCE IS WITH YOU>`, :603 `.MESS -224.,0120.,BLU,<SHOOT YOUR INITIALS>,HSZ`. So the cabinet's initials-entry prompt is literally 'SHOOT YOUR INITIALS' (HSZ) — the verb SHOOT, consistent with the aim-and-fire letter grid (GETINT / H-013). Grepped shipped tables: no 'ENTER YOUR INITIALS' string exists in the ROM. Ours draws 'ENTER YOUR INITIALS' at render.ts:1015 (verified). Genuine text divergence; the wording follows from the mechanism divergence (keyboard typing vs shoot-a-letter-grid) but the string alone is a one-line swap. Held.
